title: Upon Tap.
content:
Tap, better known than trusted, as we hear,
    Sold his old mother's spectacles for beer:
    And not unlikely; rather too than fail,
    He'll sell her eyes, and nose, for beer and ale.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Upon Tap."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
